title: 遣怀
author: 杜牧
content:
落魄江湖载酒行
楚腰纤细掌中轻
十年一觉扬州梦
赢得青楼薄幸名
translation:
Down and out, I wander the rivers and lakes with wine
Waists thin as Chu dancers, light as a swallow in the palm
Ten years — one dream of Yangzhou
All I won was a heartless reputation in the pleasure quarters
annotation: Du Mu looks back on his wild youth in Yangzhou. 'Ten years — one dream.' He's not proud, just wistful. The good times passed like a dream, and all that's left is a reputation.

title: 赠别
author: 杜牧
content:
娉娉袅袅十三余
豆蔻梢头二月初
春风十里扬州路
卷上珠帘总不如
translation:
Graceful, slender, just past thirteen
Like a cardamom bud at the tip of a branch in early spring
On the ten-mile road through Yangzhou in spring wind
Rolling up every pearl curtain — none compare to her
annotation: A 13-year-old girl, as delicate as a cardamom bud. Du Mu says: on the entire beautiful road through Yangzhou, she's the most beautiful thing. It's a love poem disguised as a farewell.

title: 金谷园
author: 杜牧
content:
繁华事散逐香尘
流水无情草自春
日暮东风怨啼鸟
落花犹似坠楼人
translation:
All the glorious things have scattered, chasing fragrant dust
The water flows without feeling, grass grows green on its own
At dusk, the east wind grieves, birds cry
Falling blossoms still resemble the woman who jumped from the tower
annotation: A wealthy man's garden, now ruined. A beautiful woman jumped from a tower for love. Now only falling blossoms remember her. Du Mu sees beauty and tragedy in every petal.

title: 出塞
author: 王昌龄
content:
秦时明月汉时关
万里长征人未还
但使龙城飞将在
不教胡马度阴山
translation:
The bright moon of Qin, the pass of Han
Ten thousand miles of march, none have returned
If only the Flying General of Dragon City were here
No barbarian horse would cross Yin Mountain
annotation: Wang Changling wishes for a great general to protect the border. It's a poem about war's endless cycle and the longing for peace.

title: 从军行
author: 王昌龄
content:
青海长云暗雪山
孤城遥望玉门关
黄沙百战穿金甲
不破楼兰终不还
translation:
Over Qinghai, long clouds darken the snow mountains
A lone city gazes toward Jade Gate Pass
Yellow sand, a hundred battles pierce golden armor
We won't return until Loulan is destroyed
annotation: Surrounded by enemies, far from home, armor worn through — but they won't give up. Wang Changling captures the soldier's oath: victory or nothing.

title: 芙蓉楼送辛渐
titlePinyin: Fu Rong Lou Song Xin Jian
titleEn: Seeing Off Xin Jian at Lotus Tower
author: 王昌龄
content:
寒雨连江夜入吴
平明送客楚山孤
洛阳亲友如相问
一片冰心在玉壶
translation:
Cold rain joins the river, night enters Wu
At dawn I see you off, Chu mountains alone
If friends in Luoyang ask about me
Tell them: an icy heart in a jade pot
annotation: Accused of wrongdoing, Wang Changling sends his friend with a message: my heart is as pure as ice in jade. One of the most famous declarations of innocence in Chinese poetry.

title: 凉州词
author: 王翰
content:
葡萄美酒夜光杯
欲饮琵琶马上催
醉卧沙场君莫笑
古来征战几人回
translation:
Grape wine in a luminous cup
About to drink, the pipa urges from horseback
Laugh not if I drunk-ly fall on the battlefield
Since ancient times, how many soldiers return?
annotation: Drink now, because tomorrow we may die. Wang Han doesn't glorify war — he shows soldiers choosing joy in the face of death. 'Laugh not if I fall drunk on the battlefield.'

title: 别董大
author: 高适
content:
千里黄云白日曛
北风吹雁雪纷纷
莫愁前路无知己
天下谁人不识君
translation:
A thousand miles of yellow clouds, the white sun dims
North wind blows, geese fly through falling snow
Don't worry about the road ahead lacking friends
Under heaven, who doesn't know you?
annotation: In the middle of a snowstorm, Gao Shi sends off a friend with the most encouraging words ever written: 'Don't worry — everyone knows you!' Sometimes that's all someone needs to hear.

title: 塞上听吹笛
titleEn: Hearing a Flute on the Frontier
author: 高适
content:
雪净胡天牧马还
月明羌笛戍楼间
借问梅花何处落
一夜关山尽望乡
translation:
Snow clears, barbarian sky, horses return home
Bright moon, Qiang flute between the watchtowers
I ask: where do the plum blossoms fall?
All night, every pass and mountain looks toward home
annotation: A flute plays a homesick melody on the frontier. The soldiers listen and think of home. 'Where do the plum blossoms fall?' — everywhere, and nowhere.

title: 逢雪宿芙蓉山主人
titlePinyin: Feng Xue Xue Fu Rong Shan Zhu Ren
titleEn: Seeking Shelter in Snow at Lotus Mountain
author: 刘长卿
content:
日暮苍山远
天寒白屋贫
柴门闻犬吠
风雪夜归人
translation:
At dusk, blue mountains far
Sky cold, white house poor
By the brush gate, dogs bark
In wind and snow, a night traveler returns
annotation: Four images, like four photographs: distant mountains, a poor house, barking dogs, a traveler in the snow. Liu Changqing tells a whole story in 20 characters.

title: 送灵澈上人
author: 刘长卿
content:
苍苍竹林寺
杳杳钟声晚
荷笠带斜阳
青山独归远
translation:
Dark, dark the bamboo forest temple
Faint, faint the evening bell
A straw hat carries the setting sun
Alone, he returns to the distant green mountains
annotation: A monk walks back to his temple at sunset. The bell fades, the sun sets, the mountains wait. Liu Changqing paints a monk's life as the most peaceful thing in the world.

title: 送崔九
author: 裴迪
content:
归山深浅去
须尽丘壑美
莫学武陵人
暂游桃源里
translation:
Return to the mountains, deep or shallow
You must exhaust all the valleys' beauty
Don't learn from the man of Wuling
Who only visited Peach Blossom Spring briefly
annotation: If you're going to retreat to the mountains, really retreat. Don't be a tourist in nature — commit to it. Pei Di's advice: go all the way.

title: 春泛若耶溪
titleEn: Spring Boat on the Ruoye Stream
author: 綦毋潜
content:
幽意无断绝
此去随所偶
晚风吹行舟
花路入溪口
际夜转西壑
隔山望南斗
潭烟飞溶溶
林月低向后
translation:
Secluded feelings, never ending
This time I'll go wherever I drift
Evening wind blows the boat
A road of flowers enters the stream mouth
Past night, turn west through ravines
Across mountains, gaze at the southern dipter
Pool mist flies, dissolving
Forest moon, low, behind
annotation: A boat drifts where it wants. Flowers line the banks, mist rises, the moon follows. This is what freedom feels like — no plan, no destination, just the stream.

title: 宿建德江
author: 孟浩然
content:
移舟泊烟渚
日暮客愁新
野旷天低树
江清月近人
translation:
I move my boat to a misty islet
At dusk, the traveler's sorrow grows new
Wilderness wide, sky lower than the trees
River clear, the moon close to people
annotation: Moored at dusk on a misty river. The sky seems to press down, but the moon feels close. Meng Haoran finds both loneliness and comfort in nature.

title: 宿桐庐江寄广陵旧游
titlePinyin: Su Tong Lu Jiang Ji Guang Ling Jiu You
titleEn: Mooring on the Tonglu River: To Old Friends in Guangling
author: 孟浩然
content:
山暝听猿愁
沧江急夜流
风鸣两岸叶
月照一孤舟
建德非吾土
维扬忆旧游
还将两行泪
遥寄海西头
translation:
Mountains dark, I hear grieving apes
The great river rushes in the night wind
Wind rustles leaves on both banks
Moon shines on one lonely boat
Jiande is not my homeland
I remember old friends in Yangzhou
I'll send these two lines of tears
Far to the west sea's edge
annotation: Far from home, listening to apes cry and rivers rush. The moon shines on his lonely boat. Meng Haoran sends his tears — literally — to friends far away.

title: 早寒有怀
author: 孟浩然
content:
木落雁南度
北风江上寒
我家襄水曲
遥隔楚云端
乡泪客中尽
孤帆天际看
迷津欲有问
平海夕漫漫
translation:
Trees shed leaves, geese fly south
North wind, the river grows cold
My home curves by the Xiang River
Far beyond Chu's clouds
Tears of homesickness, all spent in travel
A lone sail at the edge of the sky
Lost at the ford, I want to ask
The flat sea, evening stretches endlessly
annotation: Autumn cold, geese flying south, and the poet far from home. He's lost — literally at a river crossing, metaphorically in life. The endless evening sea mirrors his confusion.

title: 留别王维
author: 孟浩然
content:
寂寂竟何待
朝朝空自归
欲寻芳草去
惜与故人违
当路谁相假
知音世所稀
只应守寂寞
还掩故园扉
translation:
Silent, silent — what am I waiting for?
Morning after morning, I return empty-handed
I want to seek fragrant grass and go
But regret leaving my old friend
Who in power will help me?
True friends are rare in this world
I should just keep my loneliness
And close my garden gate
annotation: Failed in his career, Meng Haoran decides to go home. His friend Wang Wei is the only thing making him hesitate. 'True friends are rare' — so he'll go home and be alone with his garden.

title: 与诸子登岘山
titlePinyin: Yu Zhu Zi Deng Xian Shan
author: 孟浩然
content:
人事有代谢
往来成古今
江山留胜迹
我辈复登临
水落鱼梁浅
天寒梦泽深
羊公碑尚在
读罢泪沾襟
translation:
Human affairs have their rise and fall
Coming and going make the past and present
Rivers and mountains keep the great traces
Our generation climbs again
Water falls, the fish weir grows shallow
Sky cold, the Dream Marsh grows deep
Lord Yang's stele still stands
After reading it, tears soak my collar
annotation: Climbing a mountain where a great man once stood. The weir is shallow, the marsh is deep, but the monument remains. Meng Haohan weeps — not for the past, but for everyone who tried and was forgotten.

title: 渭川田家
author: 王维
content:
斜阳照墟落
穷巷牛羊归
野老念牧童
倚杖候荆扉
雉雊麦苗秀
蚕眠桑叶稀
田夫荷锄至
相见语依依
即此羡闲逸
怅然吟式微
translation:
Slanting sun shines on the village
Deep lanes, cattle and sheep return
An old countryman thinks of the shepherd boy
Leans on his stick, waits by the brush gate
Pheasants call, wheat sprouts flourish
Silkworms sleep, mulberry leaves thin
Farmers arrive with hoes
Meet and talk, reluctant to part
At this I envy their leisure
Melancholy, I sing 'Shi Wei'
annotation: Wang Wei watches farmers returning home at sunset. They're tired but content. He envies them — they have what he doesn't: a simple life with clear meaning.

title: 青溪
author: 王维
content:
言入黄花川
每逐青溪水
随山将万转
趣途无百里
声喧乱石中
色静深松里
漾漾泛菱荇
澄澄映葭苇
我心素已闲
清川澹如此
translation:
They say enter the Yellow Flower River
I always follow the green stream
Following the mountain, ten thousand turns
The journey is less than a hundred miles
Sound noisy among scattered rocks
Color still among deep pines
Rippling, floating water chestnuts
Clear, reflecting reeds
My heart has always been at leisure
The clear stream is this tranquil
annotation: A stream that twists ten thousand times but goes less than a miles as the crow flies. Wang Wei sees himself in it — busy on the outside, peaceful within.

title: 西施咏
author: 王维
content:
艳色天下重
西施宁久微
朝为越溪女
暮作吴宫君
贱日岂殊众
贵来方悟稀
邀人傅香粉
不自著衣
君宠益骄态
君怜无是非
translation:
Beauty is valued under heaven
How could Xi Shi stay obscure long?
Morning: a girl by the Yue stream
Evening: a lady in the Wu palace
In humble days, how was she different?
Only in honor did people realize her rarity
She asked others to apply her makeup
She didn't dress herself
The more the king favored her, the prouder she grew
The more he loved her, the less right or wrong mattered
annotation: Xi Shi went from washing silk to ruling a palace. But Wang Wei's real question is: when everyone treats you as special, can you still tell right from wrong?

title: 老将行
author: 王维
content:
少年十五二十时
步行夺得胡马射
射杀山中白额虎
肯数邺下黄须儿
一身转战三千里
一剑曾当百万师
汉兵奋迅如霹雳
虏骑崩腾畏蒺藜
translation:
When he was fifteen or twenty
Walking, he captured a barbarian horse
He shot and killed the white-browed tiger in the mountains
He wouldn't yield to the yellow-bearded hero of Ye
His single body fought across three thousand miles
His single sword once faced a million soldiers
Han troops charged like thunder
Barbarian cavalry crumbled, fearing caltrops
annotation: A young warrior's incredible feats — capturing horses, killing tigers, facing armies alone. But this is the beginning of a longer story: what happens when the hero grows old and forgotten?

title: 桃源行
author: 王维
content:
渔舟逐水爱山春
两岸桃花夹古津
坐看红树不知远
行尽青溪不见人
山口潜行始隈隈
山开旷望旋平陆
遥看一处攒云树
近入千家散花竹
translation:
A fishing boat follows the water, loving the mountain spring
Both banks of peach blossoms line the ancient ford
Sitting, watching red trees, not knowing the distance
Walking to the end of the green stream, seeing no one
At the mountain pass, creeping forward, winding
The mountain opens, a broad view, suddenly flat land
From afar, a place where clouds and trees gather
Up close, a thousand homes scattered with flowers and bamboo
annotation: Wang Wei retells the famous Peach Blossom Spring story. A fisherman discovers a hidden paradise. The journey is as beautiful as the destination — winding paths, sudden openings, clouds and flowers.

title: 和贾至舍人早朝大明宫之作
titlePinyin: He Jia Zhi She Ren Zao Chao Da Ming Gong Zhi Zuo
titleEn: Morning Audience at the Great Bright Palace: Matching Jia Zhi's Poem
author: 王维
content:
绛帻鸡人报晓筹
尚衣方进翠云裘
九天阊阖开宫殿
万国衣冠拜冕旒
日色才临仙掌动
香烟欲傍衮龙浮
朝罢须裁五色诏
佩声归到凤池头
translation:
Red-turbaned rooster man announces the dawn
The wardrobe official presents the green cloud robe
The nine heavens, the palace gates open
Ten thousand nations' robes and caps bow to the crown
Sunlight just touches, the immortal palm moves
Incense smoke wants to drift beside the dragon robe
After court, he must cut five-colored edicts
The sound of jade pendants returns to the Phoenix Pool
annotation: The imperial court in all its glory — dawn announcements, cloud robes, ten thousand nations bowing. Wang Wei captures the spectacle of power at sunrise.

title: 积雨辋川庄作
titlePinyin: Ji Yu Wang Chuan Zhuang Zuo
titleEn: After Persistent Rain at Wangchuan Villa
author: 王维
content:
积雨空林烟火迟
蒸藜炊黍饷东菑
漠漠水田飞白鹭
阴阴夏木啭黄鹂
山中习静观朝槿
松下清斋折露葵
野老与人争席罢
海鸥何事更相疑
translation:
Persistent rain, empty forest, smoke rises slowly
Steaming greens, cooking millet, feeding the eastern fields
Vast, vast water fields, white egrets fly
Shady, shady summer trees, yellow orioles sing
In the mountains, practicing stillness, watching morning mallows
Under pines, pure meals, picking dew-kissed mallows
The old countryman has stopped fighting for seats with people
Why should the seagulls still suspect him?
annotation: Rain, smoke, egrets, orioles — Wang Wei's mountain life is full of small beauties. The final line: he's so at peace that even the seagulls trust him. This is what enlightenment looks like.
